$comment: c77 (D-AS-REGALLOC-DIRECT-ARG-RELOAD, 2026-07-01): the sqlite func-2088 shape. A DIRECT call with 6 GPR register-args live across a high-pressure loop -> linear-scan SPILLS them -> at the call each spilled arg is reloaded into its ABI arg register. PRE-fix the rewriter reloaded each into a SCRATCH register, but SysV has only 3 non-arg caller-saved GPR scratch (rax/r10/r11) while up to 6 GPR args can spill -> exhaustion at the 4th -> error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc] (the exact func-2088 blocker c76's wide-call materialization did NOT fix, since reshaping args BEYOND the register pool never touches the IN-POOL args that spill). c77 FIX: a spilled register-passed call-arg becomes a post-regalloc SpillSlotRef operand the rewriter does NOT scratch-reload; callconv loads it DIRECTLY into its ABI arg register (frame_load argReg,[slot], sequenced as a sink in the parallel-move machinery) -> demand==supply (1 arg reg per arg) -> ZERO scratch for call args. `weighted` is self-recursive so the release Inliner leaves it a real direct call; distinct decimal weights (1,10,...,100000) make a WRONG reload change the exit code (a silent mis-reload is caught, not just a crash). ★ RED-ON-DISABLE manifests under the RELEASE pipeline: the exhaustion is a Mem2Reg-SSA-spill phenomenon (in debug the locals stay in memory and never spill), so the `release`-arm is the load-bearing witness -> revert the c77 fix and the release build emits L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc. arm64 (~30 GPRs) never spills -> value-checks the same. => 227.
